title: Research: Agent Collaboration & Communication Layer

How can agents collaborate in real-time and asynchronously? How do users collaborate THROUGH agents? Evaluate pub/sub vs queue vs event-driven patterns. Compare with OpenClaw Lane Queue model vs Strands Swarm/Graph. Research agent-to-agent notification, task handoff, shared memory, and collaborative problem-solving. Output: docs/research/collaboration/

New capabilities: browser automation, code interpreter, AWS account infra control, MCP tool marketplace. Output: docs/research/integration-enhancement/

title: Improve lead agent workflow: merge-up, research-first, document-as-you-go
description:
Update .overstory/agent-defs/lead.md with three workflow improvements:

1. MERGE-UP CONSOLIDATION: After all builders complete and pass review, the lead must merge each builder branch into its own lead branch (git merge in worktree), then send ONE merge_ready to coordinator for the lead branch. This eliminates per-builder merges at coordinator level.

2. RESEARCH-FIRST WORKFLOW: Before implementing or documenting, leads and builders should investigate, deep-dive, analyze the problem space. Read existing code/docs, use MCP research tools, understand the landscape BEFORE writing. The scout phase exists but needs to be emphasized as mandatory for complex tasks — explore first, architect second, implement third.

3. DOCUMENT-AS-YOU-GO: As implementation proceeds, builders should document their work inline — comments where non-obvious, README updates, architecture notes. This keeps things transparent and makes it easy for other agents to pick up the work. No separate documentation phase — weave it into implementation.

File to modify: .overstory/agent-defs/lead.md
Also review: .overstory/agent-defs/builder.md, scout.md for consistency
closeReason: Merged workflow improvements to lead.md, builder.md, scout.md: merge-up consolidation, research-first, document-as-you-go, periodic rebase

title: Update lead agent hooks to allow git merge/rebase for merge-up consolidation
description: The new merge-up consolidation workflow requires leads to git merge builder branches into their lead branch and periodically git rebase on main. Current PreToolUse hooks block git merge, git rebase for lead agents. Need to update hooks to allow these operations for leads.

title: Research: Skill format compatibility — OpenClaw, Claude Code, AgentCore Gateway
description:
Deep research into skill formats across platforms and how Chimera can achieve compatibility:

1. OpenClaw SKILL.md format (v1 and v2) — what does it look like, how are skills loaded/executed?
2. Claude Code skills format — how are Claude Code skills structured (frontmatter, content, triggers)?
3. MCP tools as skills — MCP servers expose tools, can these be wrapped as Chimera skills?
4. AgentCore Gateway — does it support serving/routing skills? Can it act as a skill registry?
5. Strands tools — how does Strands define and load tools? @tool decorator pattern?
6. Compatibility layer — can Chimera natively support OpenClaw SKILL.md AND Claude Code skills AND MCP tools?
7. Skill marketplace architecture — ClawHub concept, discovery, versioning, trust tiers

Output: docs/research/skills/
closeReason: Merged skill compatibility research: 4 docs (~6600 lines) covering OpenClaw/Claude Code/MCP/Strands formats, universal adapter design, marketplace architecture

title: Well-Architected Integration: 5 pillars as agent decision framework
description: Implement Well-Architected decision framework from research: (1) packages/core/src/well-architected/pillar-evaluator.ts — score infrastructure decisions against 5 pillars, (2) wa-tool-api.ts — integrate with AWS Well-Architected Tool API, (3) review-generator.ts — auto-generate Well-Architected reviews of agent-built infra, (4) tradeoff-presenter.ts — present tradeoffs to users in pillar language. Reference: docs/research/aws-account-agent/01-well-architected-framework.md

title: Autonomous Swarm Engine: vague task decomposition + blocker resolution
description: Implement autonomous problem-solving from research: (1) packages/core/src/swarm/task-decomposer.ts — break vague requests into concrete subtasks, (2) blocker-resolver.ts — detect blockers and autonomously resolve them, (3) role-assigner.ts — assign planner/researcher/builder/validator roles, (4) hitl-gateway.ts — human-in-the-loop decision points, (5) progressive-refiner.ts — POC → iterate → production refinement loop. Reference: docs/research/aws-account-agent/01-05 autonomous swarm series

title: Multi-Account + Organizations: manage sub-accounts from Chimera
description: Implement multi-account management: (1) AWS Organizations integration — list accounts, create accounts, (2) Cross-account IAM roles with external IDs, (3) Service Control Policies management, (4) Consolidated billing across accounts, (5) Cross-account resource discovery. Reference: docs/research/aws-account-agent/06-Multi-Region-Operations.md

title: Adapter registry: factory + route integration + Slack/Discord exports
description: Follow-up from platform adapters: (1) adapter registry factory in adapters/index.ts, (2) update chat.ts to select adapter by platform field, (3) add Slack/Discord exports to index.ts, (4) add TeamsChannelConfig/TelegramChannelConfig to @chimera/shared

title: Merge lead: consolidate CLI + remaining unmerged branches
description: Merge lead to consolidate: (1) builder-cli/chimera-d5b5 — 29 files, packages/cli/ full implementation, (2) Any lead branches with mulch-only content. Main conflict: CLAUDE.md. Use git show to copy NEW files and edit CLAUDE.md additively.
closeReason: CLI package packages/cli/ created via builder-cli-impl. 16 files, 1056 lines. Includes Commander.js CLI with tenant/session/skill/deploy commands, config/output utilities, 5 passing tests, monorepo integration. Known gaps noted in merge_ready (tsconfig.json ref, command naming, extra deps, missing README).
